summary: Iteration 3 — verified OCR quality bug fix on /upload (frontend-only). All 17 Playwright UI assertions pass. Language toggle renders with data-testid ocr-lang-{auto,eng,zh}; default is 'auto' (aria-checked=true, bg-amber-400 class); clicking each option correctly moves the amber pill and updates aria-checked; scan button is disabled with no files. Source-code review of /app/frontend/src/lib/ocr.js confirms: OCR_LANGS.auto.code='eng+chi_tra', OCR_LANGS.eng.code='eng', OCR_LANGS.zh.code='chi_tra' (chi_sim correctly dropped); preprocessImage() upscales images <1600px long edge, applies grayscale (0.299R+0.587G+0.114B) and 1.35x contrast around midpoint; recognize() passes tessedit_pageseg_mode=6 (uniform block), tessedit_ocr_engine_mode=1 (LSTM only), preserve_interword_spaces=1. UploadPage.startScan() correctly calls preprocessImage(file) then recognize(canvas, lang) per file, stores ocr_text/ocr_lang in sessionStorage, and fires warning toast if looksGarbled(text). Regression sanity: /home and /free-play still render with 200 responses (energy-panel, region tiles, free-play cards still working from iter 2). No new backend testing done (backend untouched per request).
test_report_links: /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iter2.xml (prior backend suite, still valid — backend untouched)
critical_code_review_comments: ocr.js recognize() uses object-literal options as the 3rd arg to Tesseract.recognize — modern tesseract.js v5 accepts these params but the recommended API is worker.setParameters() before recognize(). Current code still works but instantiates a fresh worker on every call, which downloads the language traineddata each time and is why scans feel slow. Consider a module-level createWorker() cache keyed by lang.
preprocessImage() creates ImageBitmap without closing it (bitmap.close()). Not a leak in most browsers but recommended cleanup.

looksGarbled() ratio threshold 0.6 is aggressive — a page with lots of newlines/whitespace already only counts \n and \t as good; anything else (\r, form-feed, unicode punctuation like — “ ”) is 'bad'. Consider extending the allow-list to include \r, common Unicode punctuation (0x2000–0x206F), and fullwidth ASCII (0xFF00–0xFFEF) to reduce false-positive garbled warnings for real bilingual worksheets.

startScan() sets scanning=true but on error path resets scanning=false — however on success it navigates away without resetting scanning, which is fine, but if navigation is blocked by a route guard the overlay would stick. Minor edge case.
Pre-existing (still not addressed from iter 1 & 2): /api/auth/me emits two 401 console errors on every guest page load. Gate behind a session-cookie presence check.

success_rate.backend: N/A (not tested this iteration — backend untouched)

seed_data_creation: None. Did not upload any real image (per request note that Tesseract WASM takes 10-30s and hangs the harness).
context_for_next_testing_agent: OCR bug fix (iter 3) is verified at the UI + code-path level only. Actual Tesseract recognition accuracy was NOT tested end-to-end — that requires: (1) a fixture PNG with known English text, (2) waiting ~15-30s for the WASM + 'eng' traineddata to download on first run, (3) asserting the OCR output contains the expected words. If you want to add that regression, use a canvas.toBlob() image with fillText('The quick brown fox', 40, 100) at font=48px, then trigger scan-btn with lang='eng' and wait up to 45s for /preview navigation. Also consider adding a worker cache in ocr.js so subsequent scans don't re-download traineddata (~1.5MB each).
rca of the issue: Original user report: 'OCR so rubbish, even I upload very clear English words, it still like a mess.' Root causes (all now fixed): (1) Tesseract was loading eng+chi_tra+chi_sim simultaneously — chi_tra and chi_sim overlap heavily and confuse the LSTM classifier; (2) no image preprocessing meant phone photos <1500px on the long edge had too few pixels per character for the LSTM window; (3) no PSM hint meant Tesseract used PSM=3 (auto page segmentation) which mis-segments worksheet layouts. Fix set in /app/frontend/src/lib/ocr.js addresses all three: single-language default option (English only), upscale-to-1600px + grayscale + gentle contrast, and PSM=6 + OEM=1 (LSTM only). Verified via source review that all three fixes are wired into UploadPage.startScan().
